Lower temperature for consistent classification\n197\t            )\n198\t\n199\t            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n200\t            raw = response.strip()\n201\t            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n202\t                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n203\t                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n204\t\n205\t            # Parse JSON response\n206\t            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n207\t            classifications = []\n208\t\n209\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n210\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n211\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n212\t                try:\n213\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n214\t                except ValueError:\n215\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n216\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n217\t\n218\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n219\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n220\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n221\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n222\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n223\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n224\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n225\t                )\n226\t                classifications.append(classification)\n227\t\n228\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n229\t            return classifications\n230\t\n231\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n232\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n233\t            # Fallback: return single status intent\n234\t            return [\n235\t                IntentClassification(\n236\t                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n237\t                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n238\t                    confidence=0.5,\n239\t                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n240\t                )\n241\t            ]\n242\t        except Exception as e:\n243\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n244\t            raise\n245\t\n246\t    async def route_utterance(\n247\t        self,\n248\t        utterance: str,\n249\t        utterance_id: str,\n250\t        session_id: str,\n251\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n252\t        \"\"\"\n253\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n254\t\n255\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n256\t\n257\t        Args:\n258\t            utterance: The user utterance\n259\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n260\t            session_id: Session ID\n261\t\n262\t        Returns:\n263\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n264\t        \"\"\"\n265\t        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n266\t        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n267\t        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n268\t        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n269\t        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n270\t        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n271\t\n272\t        routed_intents = []\n273\t        for classification in classifications:\n274\t            # Create intent ID\n275\t            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n276\t\n277\t            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n278\t                intent_id=intent_id,\n279\t                classification=classification,\n280\t                session_id=session_id,\n281\t                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n282\t                router_ms=router_ms,\n283\t            )\n284\t            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n285\t\n286\t        return routed_intents\n287\t\n288\t    async def process_intent(\n289\t        self,\n290\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n291\t    ) -> dict:\n292\t        \"\"\"\n293\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n294\t\n295\t        Args:\n296\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n297\t\n298\t        Returns:\n299\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n300\t        \"\"\"\n301\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n302\t\n303\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n304\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n305\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n306\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n307\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n308\t        # the dispatch itself.\n309\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n310\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n311\t\n312\t        try:\n313\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n314\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n315\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n316\t            else:\n317\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n318\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n319\t        except Exception:\n320\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n321\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n322\t            raise\n323\t\n324\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n325\t        return result\n326\t\n327\t    async def _persist_timings(\n328\t        self,\n329\t        intent_id: str,\n330\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n331\t    ) -> None:\n332\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n333\t        try:\n334\t            store = await self._get_store()\n335\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n336\t        except Exception as e:\n337\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n338\t\n339\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n340\t        self,\n341\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n342\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n343\t    ) -> dict:\n344\t        \"\"\"\n345\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n346\t\n347\t        Args:\n348\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n349\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n350\t                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n351\t\n352\t        Returns:\n353\t            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n354\t        \"\"\"\n355\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n356\t\n357\t        logger.info(\n358\t            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n359\t            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n360\t        )\n361\t\n362\t        try:\n363\t            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n364\t            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n365\t\n366\t            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n367\t            from ..registry import get_project\n368\t            repo_path = None\n369\t            ssh_target = None\n370\t            host_alias = None\n371\t            registry = get_registry()\n372\t            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n373\t                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n374\t                if entry:\n375\t                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n376\t                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n377\t                    host_alias = entry.host\n378\t                    location = entry.display_path\n379\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n380\t                else:\n381\t                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n382\t\n383\t            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n384\t            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n385\t            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n386\t            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n387\t            project_cfg = (\n388\t namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n404\t                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n405\t                ),\n406\t            )\n407\t\n408\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n409\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n410\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n411\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n412\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n413\t\n414\t            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n415\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n416\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n417\t\n418\t            fetch_result =             timings.record(\n422\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n423\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n424\t                )\n425\t\n426\t intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n430\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n431\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n432\t                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n433\t                urgency=classification.urgency,\n434\t            )\n435\t\n436\t            synth_start = timings.clock()\n437\t            synthesize_result = on the current\n440\t            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n441\t            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n442\t\n443\t            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n444\t            store = get_store()\n445\t            _topic_type_map = {\n446\t                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n447\t                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n448\t            }\n449\t            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n450\t            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n451\t                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n452\t                topic_type=topic_type,\n453\t                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n454\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n455\t            )\n456\t            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n457\t            result_id = await store.create_result(\n458\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n459\t                topic_id=topic_id,\n460\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n461\t                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n462\t                data=synthesize_result.data,\n463\t                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n464\t            )\n465\t\n466\t            return {\n467\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n468\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n469\t                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n470\t                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n471\t                \"result_id\": result_id,\n472\t                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n473\t                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n474\t                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n475\t                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n476\t                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n477\t                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n478\t            }\n479\t\n480\t        except Exception as e:\n481\t            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n482\t            return {\n483\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n484\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n485\t                \"status\": \"error\",\n486\t                \"error\": str(e),\n487\t                \"message\": \"Failed to fetch or synthesize intent\",\n488\t            }\n489\t\n490\t    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType) -> FetchIntentType:\n491\t        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n492\t        # Map enum values by string\n493\t        type_map = {\n494\t            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n495\t            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n496\t            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n497\t            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n498\t            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n499\t            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n500\t            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n506\t        self,\n507\t        bead_ref: str,\n508\t    ) -> dict | None:\n509\t        \"\"\"\n510\t        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n511\t\n512\t        Args:\n513\t            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n514\t\n515\t        Returns:\n516\t            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n517\t            if fenced, None otherwise.\n518\t        \"\"\"\n519\t        try:\n520\t            store = await self._get_store()\n521\t            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n522\t\n523\t            if not bead_watch:\n524\t                return None\n525\t\n526\t            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n527\t            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n528\t            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n529\t            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n530\t\n531\t            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n532\t                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n533\t                return {\n534\t                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n535\t                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n536\t                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n537\t                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n538\t                }\n539\t\n540\t            return None\n541\t\n542\t        except Exception as e:\n543\t            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n544\t            return None\n545\t\n546\t    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n547\t        self,\n548\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n549\t        fence_context: dict,\n550\t    ) -> dict:\n551\t        \"\"\"\n552\t        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n553\t\n554\t        Args:\n555\t            routed_intent: The routed intent\n556\t            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n557\t\n558\t        Returns:\n559\t            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n560\t        \"\"\"\n561\t        from datetime import datetime\n562\t\n563\t        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n564\t        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n565\t        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n566\t\n567\t        logger.info(\n568\t            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n569\t            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n570\t        )\n571\t\n572\t        try:\n573\t            store = await self._get_store()\n574\t\n575\t            # intent_type=\"stuck\",\n579\t                status=\"stuck\",\n580\t            )\n581\t\n582\t            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n583\t            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n584\t            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n585\t                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n586\t                topic_type=topic_type,\n587\t                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n588\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n589\t            )\n590\t\n591\t            # Link intent to topic\n592\t            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n593\t\n594\t            # Create stuck result\n595\t            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n596\t            data = {\n597\t                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n598\t                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n599\t                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n600\t                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n601\t                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n602\t                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n603\t            }\n604\t\n605\t            result_id = await store.create_result(\n606\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n607\t                topic_id=topic_id,\n608\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n609\t                summary=summary,\n610\t                data=data,\n611\t                urgency=\"high\",\n612\t            )\n613\t\n614\t            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n615\t\n616\t            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n617\t            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n618\t            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n619\t                SSEEvent(\n620\t                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n621\t                    data={\n622\t                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n623\t                        \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n624\t                        \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n625\t                        \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n626\t                        \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n627\t                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n628\t                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n629\t                    },\n630\t                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n631\t                )\n632\t            )\n633\t\n634\t            return {\n635\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n636\t                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n637\t                \"status\": \"stuck\",\n638\t                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n639\t                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n640\t                \"result_id\": result_id,\n641\t                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n642\t                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n643\t                \"message\": \"Fenced bead detected during intent routing\",\n644\t            }\n645\t\n646\t        except Exception as e:\n647\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create stuck card from fence: {e}\")\n648\t            return {\n649\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n650\t                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n651\t                \"status\": \"error\",\n652\t                \"error\": str(e),\n653\t                \"message\": \"Failed to create stuck card from fence context\",\n654\t            }\n655\t\n656\t    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n657\t        self,\n658\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n659\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n660\t    ) -> dict:\n661\t        \"\"\"\n662\t        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n663\t\n664\t        Args:\n665\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n666\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n667\t\n668\t        Returns:\n669\t            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n670\t        \"\"\"\n671\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n672\t\n673\t        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n674\t        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n675\t        try:\n676\t            store = await self._get_store()\n677\t            fenced_beads = await store.get_fenced_beads_for_session(\n678\t                routed_intent.session_id\n679\t            )\n680\t\n681\t            # If there are fenced beads, extract fence context and create stuck card\n682\t            if fenced_beads:\n683\t                # Get the most recently fenced bead\n684\t                most_recent_fenced = fenced_beads[0]\n685\t                bead_ref = most_recent_fenced[\"bead_ref\"]\n686\t                refusal_reason = most_recent_fenced.get(\"last_refusal_reason\", \"Fenced (no reason provided)\")\n687\t                refusal_count = most_recent_fenced.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n688\t\n689\t                fence_context = {\n690\t                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n691\t                    \"refusal_reason\": refusal_reason,\n692\t                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n693\t                    \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n694\t                }\n695\t\n696\t                logger.info(\n697\t                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} \"\n698\t                    f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n699\t                )\n700\t\n701\t                # Create stuck card with fence context\n702\t                return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n703\t                    routed_intent, fence_context\n704\t                )\n705\t\n706\t        except Exception as e:\n707\t            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n708\t            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n709\t\n710\t        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n711\t        # Build escalate request\n712\t        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n713\t            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n714\t            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n715\t            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n716\t            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n717\t            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n718\t            context={\n719\t                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n720\t                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n721\t            },\n722\t            metadata={\n723\t                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n724\t                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n725\t            },\n726\t        )\n727\t\n728\t        try:\n729\t            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n730\t            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n731\t            esc_start = timings.clock()\n732\t            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n733\t            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n734\t\n735\t            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n736\t            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n737\t            if result.bead_id:\n738\t                fence_context = await self._check_fence_for_bead(result.bead_id)\n739\t                if fence_context:\n740\t                    logger.info(\n741\t                        f\"Detected immediate fence for bead {result.bead_id} \"\n742\t                        f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n743\t                    )\n744\t                    # Return stuck card instead of pending card\n745\t                    return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n746\t                        routed_intent, fence_context\n747\t                    )\n748\t\n749\t            return {\n750\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n751\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n752\t                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n753\t                \"bead_id\": NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_session ON surfaces(session_id);\n48\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_state ON surfaces(state);\n49\t\n50\t-- Utterances: raw input from user\n51\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS utterances (\n52\t    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n53\t    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n54\t    raw_text    TEXT NOT NULL,\n55\t    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n56\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n57\t);\n58\t\n59\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_utterances_session ON utterances(session_id);\n60\t\n61\t-- Intents: parsed intent threads from utterances\n62\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intents (\n63\t    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n64\t    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n65\t    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n66\t    topic_id     TEXT,\n67\t    project_slug TEXT,\n68\t    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n69\t    lookup_kind  TEXT,  -- lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs' (router-emitted; see Intent Router). NULL otherwise\n70\t    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n71\t    bead_ref     TEXT,\n72\t    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n73\t    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n74\t    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n75\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n76\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n77\t);\n78\t\n79\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_session ON intents(session_id);\n80\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_topic ON intents(topic_id);\n81\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_status ON intents(status);\n82\t\n83\t-- Results: structured data returned by agents\n84\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS results (\n85\t    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n86\t    intent_id   TEXT,  -- NULL for monitoring-originated results (system-originated, no utterance)\n87\t    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n88\t    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n89\t    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n90\t    data        TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON\n91\t    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n92\t    result_type TEXT,  -- deterministic card-selector key, set at result-write time:\n93\t                       -- \"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\" for intent-derived results — one per\n94\t                       -- intent thread (the aggregated thread card); lookup threads insert the\n95\t                       -- intent's lookup_kind: \"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\";\n96\t                       -- \"monitoring:{project_slug}\" for monitoring-originated rows. The hot-path\n97\t                       -- component lookup keys on this column, no LLM (see UI-Regen Agent /\n98\t                       -- component_usage_patterns).\n99\t    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),  -- 1 when no component matched this result_type (or below threshold): the client renders the built-in generic fallback card (see Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card). 0 when a real component rendered it (card_cache holds the rendered HTML).\n100\t    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n101\t    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n102\t    acked_at    INTEGER,\n103\t    previous_result_id TEXT,  -- Link to previous result for diff\n104\t    diff_summary TEXT,  -- Human-readable diff summary\n105\t    diff_data    TEXT,  -- JSON: detailed field diffs\n106\t    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n107\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n108\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n109\t    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n110\t);\n111\t\n112\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_session ON results(session_id);\n113\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_topic ON results(topic_id);\n114\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_created ON results(created_at);\n115\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_previous ON results(previous_result_id);\n116\t\n117\t-- Topics: persistent concerns organizing intents and results\n118\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topics (\n119\t    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n120\t    label        TEXT NOT NULL,\n121\t    type         TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')) DEFAULT 'adhoc',\n122\t    project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n123\t    scope        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(scope IN ('session', 'cross-session', 'global')) DEFAULT 'session',\n124\t    session_id   TEXT,\n125\t    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n126\t    last_active  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n127\t    archived_at  INTEGER,\n128\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n129\t);\n130\t\n131\tCREATE topics(last_active DESC);\n134\t\n135\t-- Topic context cache: pre-warmed context for active topics\n136\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topic_context_cache (\n137\t    topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n138\t    context_data TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n139\t    fetched_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n140\t    expires_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n141\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n142\t);\n143\t\n144\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_context_expires ON topic_context_cache(expires_at);\n145\t\n146\t-- Intent-topic many-to-many relationship\n147\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intent_topics (\n148\t    intent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n149\t    topic_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n150\t    PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id),\n151\t    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n152\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n153\t);\n154\t\n155\t-- Feedback signals: implicit user behavior tracking for background analysis\n156\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_signals (\n157\t    signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n158\t    signal_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n159\t    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n160\t    result_id    TEXT,\n161\t    topic_id     TEXT,\n162\t    timestamp    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n163\t    data         TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n164\t    surface_type TEXT,\n165\t    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n166\t    processed_at INTEGER,\n167\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n168\t    FOREIGN KEY (result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n169\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n170\t);\n171\t\n172\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\n173\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\n174\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\n175\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n176\t\n177\t-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n178\t-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n179\t-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n180\t-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n181\t-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n182\t-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n183\t-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n184\t-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n185\t-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n186\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n187\t    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n188\t    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n189\t    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n190\t    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n191\t    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n192\t    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n193\t    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n194\t    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n195\t    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n196\t    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n197\t    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n198\t);\n199\t\n200\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n201\t\n202\t-- Bead watch table: circuit breaker tracking for async beads\n203\t-- Persistence layer for the async-path circuit breaker (plan §10 The Async Path).\n204\t-- Tracks open beads watched by the BeadWatcher, recording refusal counts,\n205\t-- last refusal reasons, SLA deadlines, and fencing state. All state is persisted\n206\t-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n207\t-- not only in memory.\n208\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n209\t    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n210\t    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n211\t    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n212\t    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n213\t    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,  -- Latest comment index processed (-1 = none)\n214\t    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n215\t    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n216\t    fenced_at          INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when bead was fenced to status=blocked (NULL if not fenced)\n217\t    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL  -- When this watch row was created\n218\t);\n219\t\n220\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch(sla_deadline);\n221\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch(fenced_at);\n222\t\"\"\"\n223\t\n224\t# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n225\t# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\n226\tDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n227\t    \"router_ms\",\n228\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n229\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n230\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n231\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n232\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n233\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n234\t    \"stt_ms\",\n235\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n236\t)\n237\t\n238\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\n239\tINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n240\t\n241\t# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n242\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n243\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n244\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n245\t    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # 6 hours for async bead-backed tasks\n246\t    \"status\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path status intents\n247\t    \"action\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path action intents\n248\t    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n249\t    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n250\t    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\n254\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\n255\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n256\t\n257\t\n258\tclass SessionStore:\n259\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n260\t\n261\t    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n262\t        self.db_path = db_path\n263\t\n264\t    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n265\t        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n266\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n267\t            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n268\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n269\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n270\t\n271\t            # Create schema\n272\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n273\t            await db.commit()\n274\t\n275\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n276\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n277\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n278\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n279\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n280\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n281\t\n282\t    @staticmethod\n283\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n284\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n285\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n286\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n287\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n288\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n289\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n290\t            await db.execute(\n291\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n292\t            )\n293\t\n294\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n295\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n296\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n297\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n298\t\n299\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n300\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n301\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n302\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n303\t\n304\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n305\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n306\t\n307\t        # Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\n308\t        await SessionStore._migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db)\n309\t\n310\t        await db.commit()\n311\t\n312\t    @staticmethod\n313\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n314\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n315\t\n316\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n317\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n318\t        \"\"\"\n319\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n320\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n321\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n322\t            status_col = None\n323\t            for col in cols:\n324\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n325\t                    status_col = col\n326\t                    break\n327\t\n328\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n329\t        if not status_col:\n330\t            return\n331\t\n332\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n333\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n334\t        async with db.execute(\n335\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n336\t        ) as cur:\n337\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n338\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n339\t                # Already migrated\n340\t                return\n341\t\n342\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n343\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n344\t\n345\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n346\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n347\t\n348\t        try:\n349\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n350\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n351\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n352\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n353\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n354\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n355\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n356\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n357\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n358\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n359\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n360\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n361\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n362\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n363\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n364\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n365\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n366\t                )\n367\t            \"\"\")\n368\t\n369\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n370\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n371\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n372\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n373\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n374\t                FROM intents\n375\t            \"\"\")\n376\t\n377\t            # Recreate indexes\n378\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n379\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n380\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n381\t\n382\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n383\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n384\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n385\t\n386\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n387\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n388\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n389\t\n390\t            await db.commit()\n391\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n392\t\n393\t        except Exception as e:\n394\t            await db.rollback()\n395\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n396\t            raise\n397\t\n398\t    @staticmethod\n399\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n400\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n401\t\n402\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n403\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n404\t        \"\"\"\n405\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n406\t        async with db.execute(\n407\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n408\t        ) as cur:\n409\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n410\t            if not table_sql:\n411\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n412\t\n413\t            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n414\t            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n415\t                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n416\t                    # Already migrated\n417\t                    return\n418\t\n419\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n420\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n421\t\n422\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n423\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n424\t\n425\t        try:\n426\t            # Create new table with nullable intent_id\n427\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n428\t                CREATE TABLE results_new (\n429\t                    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n430\t                    intent_id   TEXT,\n431\t                    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n432\t                    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n433\t                    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n434\t                    data        TEXT NOT NULL,\n435\t                    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n436\t                    result_type TEXT,\n437\t                    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),\n438\t                    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n439\t                    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n440\t                    acked_at    INTEGER,\n441\t                    previous_result_id TEXT,\n442\t                    diff_summary TEXT,\n443\t                    diff_data    TEXT,\n444\t                    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n445\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n446\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n447\t                    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n448\t                )\n449\t            \"\"\")\n450\t\n451\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n452\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n453\t                INSERT INTO results_new\n454\t                SELECT * FROM results\n455\t            \"\"\")\n456\t\n457\t            # Recreate indexes\n458\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_session\")\n459\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_topic\")\n460\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_created\")\n461\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_previous\")\n462\t\n463\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_session ON results_new(session_id)\")\n464\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_topic ON results_new(topic_id)\")\n465\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_created ON results_new(created_at)\")\n466\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_previous ON results_new(previous_result_id)\")\n467\t\n468\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n469\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE results\")\n470\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results_new RENAME TO results\")\n471\t\n472\t            await db.commit()\n473\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: results.intent_id now allows NULL\")\n474\t        except Exception as e:\n475\t            await db.rollback()\n476\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate results.intent_id: {e}\")\n477\t            raise\n478\t\n479\t    @staticmethod\n480\t    async def _migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n481\t        \"\"\"Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1.\n482\t\n483\t        The initial schema had DEFAULT 0, which meant the first comment (index 0)\n484\t        was skipped on the first check. With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n485\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n486\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n487\t        \"\"\"\n488\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the bead_watch table schema\n489\t        async with db.execute(\n490\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='bead_watch'\"\n491\t        ) as cur:\n492\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n493\t            if not table_sql:\n494\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n495\t\n496\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n497\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n498\t                # Already migrated\n499\t                return\n500\t\n501\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new default\n502\t        logger.info(\"Migrating bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\")\n503\t\n504\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n505\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n506\t\n507\t        try:\n508\t            # Create new table with DEFAULT -1\n509\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n510\t                CREATE TABLE bead_watch_new (\n511\t                    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n512\t                    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n513\t                    last_refusal_reason TEXT,\n514\t                    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,\n515\t                    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,\n516\t                    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n517\t                    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,\n518\t                    fenced_at          INTEGER,\n519\t                    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL\n520\t                )\n521\t            \"\"\")\n522\t\n523\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n524\t            # Existing rows with comment_high_water=0 are migrated as-is\n525\t            # (they represent \"comment 0 has been processed\", which is valid)\n526\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n527\t                INSERT INTO bead_watch_new\n528\t                SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n529\t            \"\"\")\n530\t\n531\t            # Recreate indexes\n532\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline\")\n533\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced\")\n534\t\n535\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch_new(sla_deadline)\")\n536\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch_new(fenced_at)\")\n537\t\n538\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n539\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE bead_watch\")\n540\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE bead_watch_new RENAME TO bead_watch\")\n541\t\n542\t            await db.commit()\n543\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: bead_watch.comment_high_water default is now -1\")\n544\t        except Exception as e:\n545\t            await db.rollback()\n546\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water: {e}\")\n547\t            raise\n548\t\n549\t    async def close(self) -> None:\n550\t        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n551\t        # one transaction.\n606\t\n607\t        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n608\t        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n609\t        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n610\t\n611\t        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n612\t        \"\"\"\n613\t        async with Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n1241\t            await db.execute(\n1242\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n1243\t                (intent_id, now),\n1244\t            )\n1245\t            if cols:\n1246\t                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n1247\t                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n1248\t                await db.execute(\n1249\t                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1250\t                    params,\n1251\t                )\n1252\t            await db.commit()\n1253\t\n1254\t    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n1255\t        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n1256\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1257\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1258\t            async with db.execute(\n1259\t                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1260\t                (intent_id,),\n1261\t            ) as cursor:\n1262\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n1263\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n1264\t\n1265\t    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n1266\t        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n1267\t\n1268\t        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n1269\t        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n1270\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n1271\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n1272\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n1273\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n1274\t\n1275\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n1276\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n1277\t        \"\"\"\n1278\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n1279\t\n1280\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n1281\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1282\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n1283\t                if since is not None:\n1284\t                    query = (\n1285\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1286\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n1287\t                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1288\t                    )\n1289\t                    params: tuple = (since,)\n1290\t                else:\n1291\t                    query = (\n1292\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1293\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1294\t                    )\n1295\t                    params = ()\n1296\t                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n1297\t                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n1298\t                if not values:\n1299\t                    continue\n1300\t                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n1301\t                result[stage] = {\n1302\t                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n1303\t                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n1304\t                    \"count\": len(values),\n1305\t                }\n1306\t        return result\n1307\t\n1308\t    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n1309\t        self,\n1310\t        session_id: str,\n1311\t        since: int | None = None,\n1312\t    ) -> dict:\n1313\t        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n1314\t        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n1315\t\n1316\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1317\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1318\t\n1319\t            # Get signal counts by type\n1320\t            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n1321\t            async with db.execute(\n1322\t                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n1323\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n1324\t                   WHERE session_id = ? Generate utterance ID if not provided\n437\t    if not utterance_id:\n438\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n439\t\n440\t    # Create utterance record\n441\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n442\t\n443\t    # Process each routed intent\n452\t        intent_results = []\n453\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n454\t            # Create intent record in store\n455\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n456\t            await store.create_intent(\n457\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n458\t                session_id=session_id,\n459\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n460\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n461\t            )\n462\t\n463\t            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n464\t            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n465\t            intent_results.append(result)\n466\t\n467\t        return {\n468\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n469\t            \"intents\": intent_results,\n470\t        }\n471\t\n472\t    except Exception as e:\n473\t        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n474\t        return JSONResponse(\n475\t            status_code=500,\n476\t            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n477\t        )\n478\t\n479\t\n480\t@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\n481\tasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n482\t    \"\"\"\n483\t    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n484\t\n485\t    Phase 0 core query loop:\n486\t    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n487\t    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n488\t    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n489\t    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n490\t\n491\t    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n492\t    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n493\t    \"\"\"\n494\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n495\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n496\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n497\t    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n498\t\n499\t    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n500\t\n501\t    store = await get_store()\n502\t    router = get_intent_router(store)\n503\t\n504\t    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n505\t    if not utterance_id:\n506\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n507\t\n508\t    # Create utterance record\n509\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n510\t\n511\t    # Route the utterance\n512\t    try:\n513\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n514\t            utterance=utterance,\n515\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n516\t            session_id=session_id,\n517\t        )\n518\t\n519\t        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n520\t        intent_tasks = []\n521\t        intent_ids = []\n522\t\n523\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n524\t            # Create intent record in store\n525\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n526\t            await store.create_intent(\n527\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n528\t                session_id=session_id,\n529\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n530\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n531\t            )\n532\t            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n533\t\n534\t            # Create task for parallel processing\n535\t            task = asyncio.create_task(\n536\t                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n537\t                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n538\t            )\n539\t            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n540\t\n541\t        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n542\t        async def stream_results():\n543\t            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n544\t            results = []\n545\t\n546\t            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n547\t                try:\n548\t                    result = await task\n549\t                    results.append(result)\n550\t\n551\t                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n552\t                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n553\t                        emit_start = time.monotonic()\n554\t                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n555\t                            SSEEvent(\n556\t                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n557\t                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n558\t                                data={\n559\t                                    \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n560\t                                    \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n561\t                                    \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n562\t                                    \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n563\t                                }\n564\t                            )\n565\t                        )\n566\t                        # Record the SSE emit cost for this intent thread\n567\t                        # (Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Non-fatal.\n568\t                        try:\n569\t                            await store.record_dispatch_timings(\n570\t                                intent_id,\n571\t                                sse_emit_ms=int((time.monotonic() - emit_start) * 1000),\n572\t                            )\n573\t                        except Exception as te:\n574\t                            logger.warning(f\"sse_emit timing not recorded for {intent_id}: {te}\")\n575\t\n576\t                except Exception as e:\n577\t                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n578\t                    results.append({\n579\t                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n580\t                        \"status\": \"error\",\n581\t                        \"error\": str(e),\n582\t                    })\n583\t\n584\t            return results\n585\t\n586\t        # Start parallel processing in background\n587\t        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n588\t\n589\t        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n590\t        return {\n591\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n592\t            \"session_id\": session_id,\n593\t            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n594\t            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n595\t            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n596\t            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n597\t        }\n598\t\n599\t    except Exception as e:\n600\t        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n601\t        return JSONResponse(\n602\t            status_code=500,\n603\t            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n604\t        )\n605\t\n606\t\n607\t@app.post(\"/api/v1/timings\")\n608\tasync def report_client_timings(request: dict):\n609\t    \"\"\"Record client-reported dispatch timings for an intent thread.\n610\t\n611\t    The server-side stages (router/fetch/synthesize/escalate/sse_emit_ms) are\n612\t    recorded by the router and the /dispatch endpoint; this endpoint is the wire\n613\t    for the two client-reported stages in the latency budget — STT final\n614\t    transcript (stt_ms) and first card render (first_render_ms) — which only the\n615\t    client can measure (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md).\n616\t    Both are nullable: a dispatch with no client reporter (the text box, the\n617\t    /api/v1/test/dispatch harness) simply never calls this and those columns\n618\t    stay NULL.\n619\t\n620\t    Body: {\"intent_id\": \"...\", \"stt_ms\": 312, \"first_render_ms\": 90}\n621\t    Either timing field is optional; intent_id is required.\n622\t    \"\"\"\n623\t    intent_id = request.get(\"intent_id\")\n624\t    if not intent_id:\n625\t        return JSONResponse(\n626\t            status_code=400,\n627\t            content={\"error\": \"intent_id is required\"},\n628\t        )\n629\t\n630\t    fields: dict[str, int] = {}\n631\t    for name in (\"stt_ms\", \"first_render_ms\"):\n632\t        value = request.get(name)\n633\t        if value is not None:\n634\t            fields[name] = int(value)\n635\t\n636\t    store = await get_store()\n637\t    try:\n638\t        # Upserts into the existing server-written row (created_at unchanged);\n639\t        # if no server row exists yet this still creates one keyed by the\n640\t        # client-reported intent_id so the timing is never lost.\n641\t        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **fields)\n642\t    except Exception as e:\n643\t        logger.warning(f\"client timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n644\t        return JSONResponse(\n645\t            status_code=500,\n646\t            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record timings: {e}\"},\n647\t        )\n648\t\n649\t    return {\"ok\": True, \"intent_id\": intent_id, \"recorded\": list(fields.keys())}\n650\t\n651\t\n652\t@app.get(\"/api/v1/timings/percentiles\")\n653\tasync def get_latency_percentiles_endpoint(since: int | None = None):\n654\t    \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per dispatch stage (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n655\t\n656\t    Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n657\t    that has at least one captured sample. The latency-baseline bead consumes\n658\t    the un-windowed store helper directly; this endpoint exposes the same\n659\t    numbers over HTTP for the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist and on-demand\n660\t    inspection. Optional ``since`` (unix timestamp) windows to recent\n661\t    dispatches only.\n662\t    \"\"\"\n663\t    store = await get_store()\n664\t    return await store.get_latency_percentiles(since=since)\n665\t\n666\t\n667\t@app.post(\"/escalate\")\n668\tasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n669\t    \"\"\"\n670\t    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead - Returns pending-card spec\n676\t    \"\"\"\n677\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n678\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n679\t    session_id = {session_id}: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n687\t\n688\t    # Create escalate request\n689\t    escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n690\t        intent_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),\n691\t }\n723\t\n724\t    except Exception as e:\n725\t        logger.error(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n726\t        return JSONResponse(\n727\t            status_code=500,\n728\t
